div.h1: Iteration: Sparse Set vs Archetype Memory Access
div.h2: Why Archetypes Are 2x+ Faster for Queries

g.text: Archetypes are ~5.3x faster for heavy iteration

div.h3: Strategy: Optimize for the Hot Path

ul.li: Sparse Sets: Best for high-frequency structural changes (adding/removing components) as it avoids data migration. Performance degrades during iteration due to random access.
Archetypes: Best for high-frequency iteration (Systems/Physics). Structural changes are expensive (requires memcpy to new table), but query execution reaches hardware limits.

g.text: All data is contiguous in memory

ul.li: Random Access: High cache miss rate on sparse lookups.
Indirection: Pointer chasing prevents CPU prefetching.
L3 Pressure: Sparse arrays often exceed L1/L2 cache size.

text.tspan: // Linear data arrays in the same table
// Linear data arrays in the same table

ul.li: Hardware Prefetch: CPU loads next cache line automatically.
SIMD: Memory is perfectly aligned for vectorization.
